task_completion.description: The 'Blobs' sample image is opened in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window titled 'blobs.gif' or similar showing the standard Blobs microscopy sample is visible in the ImageJ/Fiji workspace.

task_completion.description: The image is converted to a binary mask, which is a prerequisite for binary image arithmetic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window showing a black-and-white (binary) version of the blobs is visible, typically resulting from a Threshold operation.

task_completion.description: Binary image arithmetic is being used to isolate the voids.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Image Calculator' dialog (or equivalent binary arithmetic tool) is open, showing an operation like Subtract or XOR between two images (e.g., a filled mask and the original mask).

task_completion.description: The final image shows the internal holes as white objects on a black background, with the main particle bodies removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window displays mostly black space with small white objects corresponding exactly to the internal voids/holes originally inside the blobs.

task_completion.description: The resulting image is being saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog is open with 'void_mask.tif' entered in the file name field.

task_completion.description: The resulting image is being saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save' dialog shows the current directory path ending in 'ImageJ_Data/results/' or '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI to perform the image processing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with ImageJ menus, dialogs (like Threshold, Fill Holes, Image Calculator), and image windows, rather than terminal commands executing Python or ImageMagick scripts.

integrity.description: The final result was generated within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The isolated holes image is displayed inside a standard ImageJ image window with the typical ImageJ UI borders and zoom indicators, not just viewed in a generic file viewer.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by downloading a pre-computed result.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing 'wget', 'curl', or similar commands downloading a file named 'void_mask.tif' directly from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Blobs (25K)' sample image has verified dimensions of 256x254 pixels. The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/void_mask.tif'. A correct result will show ONLY the internal holes of the blobs as white pixels on a black background. The white pixels (foreground) should constitute approximately 0.5% to 10% of the total image area (e.g., around 6.7% if a threshold of 40 was used).
